```text
35	// Check verifies that DeepSeek TUI is available and returns its resolved

156	// for session updates.
157	func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
158		if sessionCallback == nil {
159			return ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
160		}
161	
162		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
163		defer cancel()
164	
165		watchErr := make(chan error, 1)
166		go func() {
167			watchErr <- watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
168		}()
169	
170		err := ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
171		cancel()
172	
173		if werr := <-watchErr; werr != nil && !errors.Is(werr, context.Canceled) {
174			slog.Warn("deepseek: watcher stopped with error", "error", werr)
175		}
176	
177		if err != nil {
178			return fmt.Errorf("deepseek execution failed: %w", err)
179		}
180		return nil
181	}

184	// sessionCallback for each new or updated session until ctx is canceled.
185	func (p *Provider) WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
186		if sessionCallback == nil {

188		}

222	// --- helpers shared across the package ---
223	
224	func classifyCheckError(err error) string {
225		var execErr *exec.Error
226		var pathErr *os.PathError
227		switch {
228		case errors.As(err, &execErr) && execErr.Err == exec.ErrNotFound:
229			return "not_found"
230		case errors.As(err, &pathErr) && errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrPermission):
231			return "permission_denied"
232		case errors.Is(err, os.ErrPermission):
233			return "permission_denied"
234		default:
235			return "version_failed"
236		}
237	}
238	
239	func buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType string, command string, isCustom bool, stderr string) string {
240		var builder strings.Builder
241		switch errorType {
242		case "not_found":
243			builder.WriteString("DeepSeek TUI was not found.\n\n")
244			if isCustom {
245				builder.WriteString("• Verify the custom path you provided is executable.\n")
246				fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "• Provided command: %s\n", command)
247			} else {
248				builder.WriteString("• Install DeepSeek TUI and ensure `deepseek` is on your PATH.\n")
249				builder.WriteString("• Re-run `specstory check deepseek` after installation.\n")
250			}
251		case "permission_denied":
252			builder.WriteString("SpecStory cannot execute DeepSeek TUI due to permissions.\n\n")
253			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Try: chmod +x %s\n", command)
254		default:
255			builder.WriteString("`deepseek --version` failed.\n\n")
256			if stderr != "" {
257				builder.WriteString("Error output:\n")
258				builder.WriteString(stderr)
259				builder.WriteString("\n\n")
260			}
261			builder.WriteString("Run `deepseek --version` manually to diagnose, then retry.")
262		}
263		return builder.String()
264	}
265	
266	func trackCheckSuccess(provider string, custom bool, commandPath string, resolvedPath string, version string, versionFlag string) {
267		props := analytics.Properties{
268			"provider":       provider,
269			"custom_command": custom,
270			"command_path":   commandPath,
271			"resolved_path":  resolvedPath,
272			"version":        version,
273			"version_flag":   versionFlag,
274		}
275		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallSuccess, props)
276	}
277	
278	func trackCheckFailure(provider string, custom bool, commandPath string, resolvedPath string, versionFlag string, stderrOutput string, errorType string, message string) {
279		props := analytics.Properties{
280			"provider":       provider,
281			"custom_command": custom,
282			"command_path":   commandPath,
283			"resolved_path":  resolvedPath,
284			"version_flag":   versionFlag,
285			"error_type":     errorType,
286			"error_message":  message,
287		}
288		if stderrOutput != "" {
289			props["stderr"] = stderrOutput
290		}
291		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, props)
292	}
```

```text
1	package deepseek
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"sort"
9		"strings"
10	
11		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		deepSeekRootDir     = ".deepseek"
16		deepSeekSessionsDir = "sessions"
17	)
18	
19	type sessionFile struct {
20		Path     string
21		ModTime  int64
22		FileName string
23	}
24	
25	// resolveSessionsDir returns the path to DeepSeek TUI's sessions directory.
26	func resolveSessionsDir() (string, error) {
27		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
28		if err != nil {
29			return "", fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot resolve home dir: %w", err)
30		}
31		return filepath.Join(home, deepSeekRootDir, deepSeekSessionsDir), nil
32	}
33	
34	// listSessionFiles returns all JSON session files in ~/.deepseek/sessions/,
35	// sorted by modification time (newest first).
36	func listSessionFiles() ([]sessionFile, error) {
37		dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
38		if err != nil {
39			return nil, err
40		}
41	
42		entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
43		if err != nil {
44			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
45				return []sessionFile{}, nil
46			}
47			return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: unable to read sessions dir: %w", err)
48		}
49	
50		var files []sessionFile
51		for _, entry := range entries {
52			if entry.IsDir() {
53				continue
54			}
55			if filepath.Ext(entry.Name()) != ".json" {
56				continue
57			}
58	
59			info, err := entry.Info()
60			if err != nil {
61				continue
62			}
63	
64			files = append(files, sessionFile{
65				Path:     filepath.Join(dir, entry.Name()),
66				ModTime:  info.ModTime().UnixNano(),
67				FileName: entry.Name(),
68			})
69		}
70	
71		// Sort newest first.
72		sort.Slice(files, func(i, j int) bool {
73			return files[i].ModTime > files[j].ModTime
74		})
75	
76		return files, nil
77	}
78	
79	// findSessionFileByID finds a session file by its ID (UUID).
80	// The ID should match a filename like <uuid>.json.
81	func findSessionFileByID(sessionID string) (string, error) {
82		files, err := listSessionFiles()
83		if err != nil {
84			return "", err
85		}
86	
87		for _, file := range files {
88			candidate := trimExt(file.FileName)
89			if candidate == sessionID || file.FileName == sessionID {
90				return file.Path, nil
91			}
92		}
93		return "", nil
94	}
95	
96	func trimExt(name string) string {
97		ext := filepath.Ext(name)
98		if ext == "" {
99			return name
100		}
101		return name[:len(name)-len(ext)]
102	}
103	
104	// sessionMentionsProject checks whether a session file's workspace metadata
105	// matches the given project path.
106	func sessionMentionsProject(filePath string, projectPath string) bool {
107		projectPath = strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
108		if projectPath == "" {
109			return false
110		}
111	
112		// Quick check: read only the metadata portion of the JSON to avoid
113		// parsing the full messages array.
114		workspace := extractWorkspaceFast(filePath)
115		if workspace == "" {
116			return false
117		}
118	
119		canonicalProject := canonicalizePath(projectPath)
120		canonicalWorkspace := canonicalizePath(workspace)
121	
122		return canonicalProject == canonicalWorkspace
123	}
124	
125	// extractWorkspaceFast reads just the workspace field from a session file
126	// without parsing the full JSON. The metadata block is at the top of the file,
127	// so we stream and bail on the first match — important on the watcher hot
128	// path where this is called per fs event.
129	func extractWorkspaceFast(filePath string) string {
130		f, err := os.Open(filePath)
131		if err != nil {
132			return ""
133		}
134		defer func() { _ = f.Close() }()
135	
136		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)
137		// DeepSeek session files can contain individual lines well over the default
138		// 64KB scanner limit (long tool_result blobs); raise to 1MB which still
139		// bounds memory but covers everything seen in practice.
140		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 1024*1024)
141		for scanner.Scan() {
142			line := scanner.Text()
143			messagesIdx := strings.Index(line, `"messages"`)
144			searchLine := line
145			if messagesIdx >= 0 {
146				searchLine = line[:messagesIdx]
147			}
148			if ws := matchWorkspace(searchLine); ws != "" {
149				return ws
150			}
151			// Once we cross into the messages array the metadata is behind us.
152			if messagesIdx >= 0 {
153				return ""
154			}
155		}
156		return ""
157	}
158	
159	// matchWorkspace pulls the value out of a `"workspace": "<value>"` JSON line.
160	func matchWorkspace(line string) string {
161		idx := strings.Index(line, `"workspace"`)
162		if idx < 0 {
163			return ""
164		}
165		rest := line[idx+len(`"workspace"`):]
166		colon := strings.Index(rest, ":")
167		if colon < 0 {
168			return ""
169		}
170		rest = strings.TrimSpace(rest[colon+1:])
171		if len(rest) == 0 || rest[0] != '"' {
172			return ""
173		}
174		rest = rest[1:]
175		end := strings.Index(rest, `"`)
176		if end < 0 {
177			return ""
178		}
179		return rest[:end]
180	}
181	
182	// canonicalizePath resolves a path to its canonical form for comparison.
183	func canonicalizePath(path string) string {
184		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(path)
185		if trimmed == "" {
186			return ""
187		}
188		canonical, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(trimmed)
189		if err == nil {
190			return canonical
191		}
192		if abs, err := filepath.Abs(trimmed); err == nil {
193			return filepath.Clean(abs)
194		}
195		return filepath.Clean(trimmed)
196	}
197
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 13:49:41Z)**_

I found the issue. Here's the diagnosis.

**The session file is in a subdirectory the provider doesn't scan.**

Your DeepSeek session is here:

```
~/.deepseek/sessions/checkpoints/latest.json
```

…with `workspace: "/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/compositions/deepseek-1"` — which would match exactly.

But the provider's `listSessionFiles()` in `pkg/providers/deepseek/path_utils.go:36-77` only reads the **top level** of `~/.deepseek/sessions/`:

- `os.ReadDir(dir)` doesn't recurse
- `if entry.IsDir() { continue }` (line 52-54) explicitly skips subdirectories
- And it only accepts files with extension `.json` directly under `sessions/`

So `checkpoints/latest.json` is invisible to the scan, and `DetectAgent` falls through to the "no activity found" warning. The same blind spot affects `GetAgentChatSessions`, `ListAgentChatSessions`, and the watcher.

**Why this is happening now:** DeepSeek appears to have changed (or your version uses) a layout where active sessions are stored as `sessions/checkpoints/latest.json` rather than `sessions/<uuid>.json` flat. Top-level `sessions/` is empty for you — only the `checkpoints/` subdir exists.

**Options to fix:**

1. **Recurse into `sessions/` and pick up nested `.json` files** — most permissive, would handle whatever DeepSeek throws at us, but risks picking up unrelated state files if DeepSeek adds more subdirs in future.
2. **Explicitly also scan `sessions/checkpoints/*.json`** — narrowest, but if `latest.json` is the only file that ever lives there it's racy: the file is repeatedly rewritten as "latest" rather than being a stable per-session artifact, so we'd need to dedupe by `metadata.id` rather than path.
3. **Walk `sessions/` with `filepath.WalkDir` filtering to `.json`** — middle ground; recursive but bounded to the sessions tree.

The fact that the file is called `checkpoints/latest.json` (size 75KB, 10 messages, freshly written) strongly suggests DeepSeek writes the current session as a single rolling "latest" file rather than per-session files keyed by UUID. If that's right, option 2 needs ID-based dedupe, and any of these options needs the watcher to handle the file being overwritten in place rather than created new.

My recommendation: confirm with a second DeepSeek run whether `latest.json` is overwritten or whether a new file appears (a previous-session file in `checkpoints/`, or a new file elsewhere). That tells us whether DeepSeek persists history at all, or only the current session — which changes the fix substantially. Want me to dig into how `agent_session.go` parses session IDs to confirm whether a `latest.json`-only world would even work with sync/dedupe as written?

```text
    /// Create a new `SessionManager` with the specified sessions directory
    pub fn new(sessions_dir: PathBuf) -> std::io::Result<Self> {
        let sessions_dir = normalize_managed_dir(sessions_dir)?;
        // Ensure the sessions directory exists
        fs::create_dir_all(&sessions_dir)?;
        Ok(Self { sessions_dir })
    }

    /// Create a `SessionManager` using the default location (~/.deepseek/sessions)
    pub fn default_location() -> std::io::Result<Self> {
        Self::new(default_sessions_dir()?)
    }

    /// Save a session to disk using atomic write (temp file + fsync + rename).
    pub fn save_session(&self, session: &SavedSession) -> std::io::Result<PathBuf> {
        let path = self.validated_session_path(&session.metadata.id)?;

        let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(session)
            .map_err(|e| std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, e))?;

        // Atomic write via write_atomic (NamedTempFile + fsync + persist)
        write_atomic(&path, content.as_bytes())?;

        // Clean up old sessions if we have too many
        self.cleanup_old_sessions()?;

        Ok(path)
    }

    /// Save a crash-recovery checkpoint for in-flight turns.
    pub fn save_checkpoint(&self, session: &SavedSession) -> std::io::Result<PathBuf> {
        let checkpoints = self.sessions_dir.join("checkpoints");
        fs::create_dir_all(&checkpoints)?;
        let path = checkpoints.join("latest.json");
        let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(session)
            .map_err(|e| std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, e))?;
        write_atomic(&path, content.as_bytes())?;
```

```text
                // Write coalesced work.
                if should_clear {
                    let _ = manager.clear_checkpoint();
                    should_clear = false;
                }
                if let Some(ref session) = latest_checkpoint.take() {
                    let _ = manager.save_checkpoint(session);

                // Block until the next request arrives.
                match rx.recv().await {
                    Some(PersistRequest::Checkpoint(session)) => {
                        latest_checkpoint = Some(session);
                    }
                    Some(PersistRequest::SessionSnapshot(session)) => {
                        latest_session = Some(session);

                    Some(PersistRequest::ClearCheckpoint) => {
                        should_clear = true;

                    Some(PersistRequest::Shutdown) => {
                        flush_inner(
                            &manager,
                            latest_checkpoint.as_ref(),
                            latest_session.as_ref(),
                            should_clear,
                        );
                        return;
                    }
                    None => {
                        // Channel closed — final flush and exit.
                        flush_inner(
                            &manager,
                            latest_checkpoint.as_ref(),
                            latest_session.as_ref(),
                            should_clear,
                        );
                        return;
                    }
                }
            }
        },
    );

    handle
}

/// Write any pending work to disk (used on shutdown).
fn flush_inner(
    manager: &SessionManager,
    checkpoint: Option<&SavedSession>,
    session: Option<&SavedSession>,
    should_clear: bool,
) {
    if should_clear {
        let _ = manager.clear_checkpoint();
    }
    if let Some(s) = checkpoint {
        let _ = manager.save_checkpoint(s);
    }
    if let Some(s) = session {
        let _ = manager.save_session(s);
    }
}
```

```text
    /// Load the most recent crash-recovery checkpoint if present.
    pub fn load_checkpoint(&self) -> std::io::Result<Option<SavedSession>> {
        let path = self.sessions_dir.join("checkpoints").join("latest.json");
        if !path.exists() {
            return Ok(None);
        }
        let content = fs::read_to_string(&path)?;
        let session: SavedSession = serde_json::from_str(&content)
            .map_err(|e| std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, e))?;
        if session.schema_version > CURRENT_SESSION_SCHEMA_VERSION {
            return Err(std::io::Error::new(
                std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidData,
                format!(
                    "Checkpoint schema v{} is newer than supported v{}",
                    session.schema_version, CURRENT_SESSION_SCHEMA_VERSION
                ),
            ));
        }
        Ok(Some(session))
    }

    /// Clear any crash-recovery checkpoint.
    pub fn clear_checkpoint(&self) -> std::io::Result<()> {
        let path = self.sessions_dir.join("checkpoints").join("latest.json");
        if path.exists() {
            fs::remove_file(path)?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Save offline queue state (queued + draft messages).
    pub fn save_offline_queue_state(
        &self,
        state: &OfflineQueueState,
        session_id: Option<&str>,
    ) -> std::io::Result<PathBuf> {
        let checkpoints = self.sessions_dir.join("checkpoints");
        fs::create_dir_all(&checkpoints)?;
        let path = checkpoints.join("offline_queue.json");
        let mut state_with_id = state.clone();
        state_with_id.session_id = session_id.map(|s| s.to_string());
        let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&state_with_id)
            .map_err(|e| std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, e))?;
        write_atomic(&path, content.as_bytes())?;
        Ok(path)
    }
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 13:52:08Z)**_

Here's the complete answer from DeepSeek's source.

**DeepSeek TUI has two distinct on-disk session files with different lifecycles:**

`crates/tui/src/session_manager.rs:238-289`:

- **Persisted session** — `~/.deepseek/sessions/<uuid>.json`. Written by `save_session()`. This is the canonical, durable record.
- **Crash-recovery checkpoint** — `~/.deepseek/sessions/checkpoints/latest.json`. Written by `save_checkpoint()`. Transient. There's only ever one — it rolls forward as the session progresses.

**When each is written** (`crates/tui/src/tui/ui.rs:1321-1327` and `crates/tui/src/tui/persistence_actor.rs:142-145`):

1. Mid-turn (assistant streaming, tool approval pending, etc.) → only `checkpoints/latest.json` is updated repeatedly.
2. Turn **completes** → `save_session()` writes `<uuid>.json`, then `ClearCheckpoint` deletes `checkpoints/latest.json`.

So in steady state after a completed turn: only `<uuid>.json` exists. While a turn is in flight: only `checkpoints/latest.json` exists.

**Your specific case** — your audit log at `~/.deepseek/audit.log` ends with:

```
2026-05-13T13:46:17  tool.approval.prompted  task_shell_start  session_id a1e76ea8…
```

…and then nothing. The TUI was still waiting on you to approve a `task_shell_start` tool call when you ran `specstory sync` at 13:47. The turn never completed, so `save_session()` was never invoked, so no `<uuid>.json` was ever produced. All that exists is the crash-recovery checkpoint — exactly matching `metadata.id = a1e76ea8…` from the audit log.

**So the difference comes from this:** our provider reads only top-level `<uuid>.json` files (the persisted-session format), which is the right thing to do for completed sessions. But it has no awareness of `checkpoints/latest.json`, so any in-flight DeepSeek session is invisible to `specstory sync`. The moment you complete (or kill) the turn cleanly via DeepSeek finishing its response, DeepSeek will write `a1e76ea8-….json` to the top level and `specstory sync` will find it.

**Implications for the SpecStory provider:**

- Reading from `checkpoints/latest.json` is risky — DeepSeek owns that file with atomic-rename writes, and it gets deleted the moment a turn completes. We'd be racing it.
- A turn-finished `<uuid>.json` and a mid-turn `checkpoints/latest.json` for the same session have the same `metadata.id`, so dedupe is straightforward if we ever want to surface in-flight sessions.
- There's also `MAX_SESSIONS` cleanup (`session_manager.rs:469-481`) — DeepSeek prunes its own oldest sessions, which we should be aware of for sync staleness but probably doesn't need a code change.
- A separate `checkpoints/offline_queue.json` exists (line 302) for queued messages when offline. Not a session file; ignore it.

**My recommendation:** Don't change the provider's scanning strategy. The current behaviour is correct — wait for DeepSeek to finalize the session to its canonical location. The user-facing fix is improving the "no activity found" warning when we detect a `checkpoints/latest.json` whose workspace matches the project: tell the user the session is still in-flight and to finish or exit the DeepSeek turn first. Want me to draft that warning change?

```text
/// Load a recent crash-recovery checkpoint, pruning stale checkpoints first.
fn load_recent_checkpoint(
    manager: &session_manager::SessionManager,
) -> Option<(session_manager::SavedSession, std::time::Duration)> {
    let session = manager.load_checkpoint().ok().flatten()?;

    let home = dirs::home_dir()?;
    let checkpoint_path = home
        .join(".deepseek")
        .join("sessions")
        .join("checkpoints")
        .join("latest.json");
    let metadata = std::fs::metadata(&checkpoint_path).ok()?;
    let mtime = metadata.modified().ok()?;
    let age = std::time::SystemTime::now().duration_since(mtime).ok()?;
    if age > std::time::Duration::from_secs(24 * 3600) {
        let _ = manager.clear_checkpoint();
        return None;
    }

    Some((session, age))
}

fn checkpoint_age_label(age: std::time::Duration) -> String {
    if age.as_secs() < 60 {
        format!("{}s ago", age.as_secs())
    } else if age.as_secs() < 3600 {
        format!("{}m ago", age.as_secs() / 60)
    } else {
        format!("{}h ago", age.as_secs() / 3600)
    }
}

/// Check for a crash-recovery checkpoint and return the session ID if explicit
/// recovery was requested *and* the checkpoint belongs to the current
/// workspace.
///
/// The checkpoint must exist and its file mtime must be within 24 hours.
/// **The checkpoint's workspace must also match the resolved launch workspace
/// after canonicalisation.** If the workspace doesn't match, the checkpoint is
/// persisted as a regular session (so the user can find it via
/// `deepseek sessions` / `deepseek resume <id>`) and cleared, but not loaded.
fn recover_interrupted_checkpoint_for_resume(launch_workspace: &Path) -> Option<String> {
    let manager = session_manager::SessionManager::default_location().ok()?;
    let (session, age) = load_recent_checkpoint(&manager)?;

    // Refuse to silently restore a session from another workspace. Compare
    // against the resolved launch workspace, not the shell cwd, so callers
    // using `--workspace` cannot accidentally recover a checkpoint from the
    // directory their shell happened to be in.
    let session_workspace = session.metadata.workspace.clone();
    let workspace_matches =
        session_manager::workspace_scope_matches(&session_workspace, launch_workspace);

    if !workspace_matches {
        // Persist the checkpoint so the user can find it via `deepseek
        // sessions`, then clear it so the next launch in this folder doesn't
        // re-trip the nag. Print a one-line notice pointing at the explicit
        // resume command — but DO NOT auto-load the session here.
        let _ = manager.save_session(&session);
        let _ = manager.clear_checkpoint();
        eprintln!(
            "Note: an interrupted session from another workspace ({}) is \
             available. Run `deepseek sessions` to list saved sessions. Starting \
             fresh in {}.",
            session_workspace.display(),
            launch_workspace.display(),
        );
        return None;
    }

    let session_id = session.metadata.id.clone();

    // Persist the checkpoint as a regular session so the TUI can load it by id.
    if manager.save_session(&session).is_err() {
        return None;
    }

    // Clear the checkpoint now that it has been recovered.
    let _ = manager.clear_checkpoint();

    let age_str = checkpoint_age_label(age);
    eprintln!("Recovered interrupted session ({age_str}). Use --fresh to start fresh.",);

    Some(session_id)
}

    if session_manager::workspace_scope_matches(&session_workspace, launch_workspace) {
```

```text
### Changed
- **Plain `deepseek` starts a fresh session** - opening a second `deepseek` in
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 13:55:49Z)**_

Confirmed — no locking on the checkpoint path. Here's what happens.

**There is only one `checkpoints/latest.json`, full stop.** Path is hardcoded at `session_manager.rs:255-257` (`save_checkpoint`) and again at `:266` (`load_checkpoint`) and `:287` (`clear_checkpoint`). No PID, no session ID, no workspace hash. Every running TUI writes to the same file.

**While both sessions are running**, each one's persistence actor coalesces its own pending state and periodically calls `save_checkpoint()` (`persistence_actor.rs:142`), which is an atomic temp-file-plus-rename (`session_manager.rs:246`). So `latest.json` is whichever TUI wrote last — it just keeps flipping between the two sessions' snapshots. No corruption (atomic rename), but the content is constantly being overwritten with the wrong session's state.

**The disasters happen at the boundaries:**

1. **Either session completes a turn cleanly** → its persistence actor sends `ClearCheckpoint` (`ui.rs:1327`), which calls `fs::remove_file(checkpoints/latest.json)` unconditionally (`session_manager.rs:286-292`). The *other* session's in-flight checkpoint is deleted along with it. The persisted `<uuid>.json` for the completed session is fine, but the in-flight one loses its crash-recovery insurance entirely until its next `save_checkpoint()` tick re-creates the file.

2. **Either session crashes** → next time you launch DeepSeek and ask for recovery (`--continue`), the recovery path reads `checkpoints/latest.json` (`main.rs:3976-3997`) and gets whichever session happened to write last. If that's session A but session B is the one you wanted, B is gone. There's a 24-hour staleness check and a workspace-match check (`main.rs:4017-4029`), so a wrong-workspace checkpoint won't auto-attach — instead it's persisted as a regular session and the checkpoint cleared. Still: **only one of the two crashed sessions is recoverable; the other is lost.**

3. **The CHANGELOG at line 1985 explicitly acknowledges this design choice:** "Plain `deepseek` starts a fresh session — opening a second `deepseek` in the same folder no longer silently attaches to the same in-flight checkpoint." That fix addresses the *auto-attach* footgun (two TUIs both thinking they own the checkpoint) but it does not address concurrent writers stomping each other. The `--fresh` path still flows through `preserve_interrupted_checkpoint_for_explicit_resume` (`main.rs:4063+`) which calls `save_session()` on the current checkpoint then clears it — so launching a second TUI in the same folder will **persist the first TUI's in-flight checkpoint as a regular session** (potentially mid-turn, with whatever state it had captured) and **wipe** the first TUI's checkpoint, while the first TUI is still running. The first TUI keeps going and writes a new checkpoint on its next tick, so it's not fatal — but you now have a half-finished `<uuid>.json` of session A in the durable store that session A is still actively writing to.

**Workspace check doesn't save you across sessions in the same workspace.** `workspace_scope_matches` (referenced at `main.rs:4032`) gates *recovery*, not *writes*. Two TUIs in the same folder hammer the same file regardless.

**Implications for our SpecStory provider:**

- If we ever surface `checkpoints/latest.json` to users, we cannot assume it represents a stable session — its identity flips between concurrent sessions tick-by-tick. Reading it once and matching `metadata.id` is the only safe way to know which session you got.
- Detecting "in-flight" is fundamentally racy: between us reading `latest.json` and parsing it, DeepSeek may have rewritten it (atomic rename means we won't see a torn file, but we might see session A's checkpoint and a millisecond later it's session B's).
- The right product story is: **wait for `<uuid>.json` to appear in the top level.** That's the only stable, durable, multi-session-safe artifact. Our current behaviour is correct, and the only improvement worth making is the warning-message change I suggested in the previous turn — but now with the caveat that for multi-session users, the warning is at best a best-effort hint about *one* of their in-flight sessions.

Net answer to your question: **two concurrent DeepSeek TUIs share one checkpoint file with no locking, no per-instance separation, and a "last writer wins" + "first completer deletes everyone's" lifecycle.** It's a known sharp edge in DeepSeek — partially mitigated by the "fresh by default" change in their changelog, but not eliminated.

```bash
/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 <<'PY'
import re, json
md_path = "/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/specstory-cli-multi-agent/deepseek-tui/2026-05-13/.specstory/history/2026-05-13_14-35-59Z-hello-deepseek-tui-tell.md"
sd_path = "/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/specstory-cli-multi-agent/deepseek-tui/2026-05-13/.specstory/debug/2429dd93-45df-4ea2-a42c-cbb56918ff6b/session-data.json"

# Index markdown real tool-uses (no leading whitespace)
lines = open(md_path).read().splitlines()
md_tools = []
open_idx = None
open_name = None
open_type = None
for i, l in enumerate(lines, 1):
    if l.startswith("<tool-use"):
        m = re.search(r'data-tool-name="([^"]+)"', l)
        t = re.search(r'data-tool-type="([^"]+)"', l)
        open_idx = i
        open_name = m.group(1) if m else "?"
        open_type = t.group(1) if t else "?"
    elif l.startswith("</details></tool-use>") and open_idx:
        md_tools.append((open_idx, i, open_name, open_type))
        open_idx = None

# Index session-data tool entries with line numbers
sd_lines = open(sd_path).read().splitlines()
sd_tools = []
i = 0
while i < len(sd_lines):
    l = sd_lines[i]
    # detect "tool": { line
    if l.strip().startswith('"tool"') and l.rstrip().endswith('{'):
        tool_start = i + 1  # 1-indexed
        # Find name and useId on next lines
        name = useid = None
        j = i + 1
        depth = 1
        while j < len(sd_lines):
            inner = sd_lines[j]
            if '"name":' in inner and name is None:
                m = re.search(r'"name":\s*"([^"]+)"', inner)
                if m: name = m.group(1)
            if '"useId":' in inner and useid is None:
                m = re.search(r'"useId":\s*"([^"]+)"', inner)
                if m: useid = m.group(1)
            depth += inner.count('{') - inner.count('}')
            if depth == 0:
                break
            j += 1
        sd_tools.append((tool_start, j+1, name, useid))
        i = j+1
    else:
        i += 1

print(f"Markdown real tool-uses: {len(md_tools)}")
print(f"Session-data tools: {len(sd_tools)}")

# Verify name sequence matches
mismatch = []
for k, (mt, st) in enumerate(zip(md_tools, sd_tools)):
    if mt[2] != st[2]:
        mismatch.append((k, mt[2], st[2]))
print(f"Name mismatches: {len(mismatch)}")
if mismatch:
    for m in mismatch[:10]:
        print("  ", m)

# Write paired index
with open("/tmp/audit_pairs.tsv", "w") as f:
    f.write("idx\tmd_start\tmd_end\ttype\tname\tsd_start\tsd_end\tuseid\n")
    for k, (mt, st) in enumerate(zip(md_tools, sd_tools), 1):
        f.write(f"{k}\t{mt[0]}\t{mt[1]}\t{mt[3]}\t{mt[2]}\t{st[0]}\t{st[1]}\t{st[3]}\n")

# Also count by tool name
from collections import Counter
c = Counter(t[2] for t in md_tools)
print("\nTools by frequency:")
for name, n in c.most_common():
    print(f"  {n:3d}  {name}")
PY
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 16:20:01Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Re-index session-data tools robustly

```bash
/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 <<'PY'
import re, json
sd_path = "/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/specstory-cli-multi-agent/deepseek-tui/2026-05-13/.specstory/debug/2429dd93-45df-4ea2-a42c-cbb56918ff6b/session-data.json"

# Parse the JSON properly to get all tools with line ranges. We need to track
# both objects in the JSON tree AND their line positions. Easiest: find every
# `"tool":` key occurrence and grab the name + useId from the following lines
# (since session-data.json is pretty-printed).
sd_lines = open(sd_path).read().splitlines()

sd_tools = []
for i, l in enumerate(sd_lines):
    stripped = l.lstrip()
    if stripped.startswith('"tool":') and stripped.endswith('{'):
        start = i + 1  # 1-indexed
        # Look ahead for name + useId
        name = useid = None
        # Object close is the line with matching whitespace ending in "}"
        indent = len(l) - len(l.lstrip())
        end = None
        for j in range(i+1, len(sd_lines)):
            ln = sd_lines[j]
            if '"name":' in ln and name is None:
                m = re.search(r'"name":\s*"([^"]+)"', ln)
                if m: name = m.group(1)
            if '"useId":' in ln and useid is None:
                m = re.search(r'"useId":\s*"([^"]+)"', ln)
                if m: useid = m.group(1)
            # closing brace at same indent ends the tool object
            if len(ln) - len(ln.lstrip()) == indent and ln.lstrip().startswith('}'):
                end = j + 1
                break
        sd_tools.append((start, end, name, useid))

print(f"Session-data tools: {len(sd_tools)}")
# Show first 5 and last 5
for t in sd_tools[:5]:
    print(t)
print("...")
for t in sd_tools[-5:]:
    print(t)

# Save
with open("/tmp/sd_tools.tsv", "w") as f:
    f.write("idx\tsd_start\tsd_end\tname\tuseid\n")
    for k, t in enumerate(sd_tools, 1):
        f.write(f"{k}\t{t[0]}\t{t[1]}\t{t[2]}\t{t[3]}\n")
PY
```

```bash
/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 <<'PY'
import re
# Build representative list: one per unique tool name (first occurrence)
pairs = []
with open("/tmp/pairs.tsv") as f:
    next(f)
    for line in f:
        idx, typ, name, mstart, mend, sstart, send, uid = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
        pairs.append({"idx": int(idx), "type": typ, "name": name,
                       "ms": int(mstart), "me": int(mend),
                       "ss": int(sstart), "se": int(send), "uid": uid})

# Group by name
groups = {}
for p in pairs:
    groups.setdefault(p["name"], []).append(p)

# Print a sample plan: one block per name (first occurrence)
print(f"Unique tools: {len(groups)}")
for name, items in groups.items():
    first = items[0]
    span = first["me"] - first["ms"] + 1
    print(f"  {name:30s} count={len(items):2d} first md={first['ms']}-{first['me']} ({span} lines)")
PY
```

Build the audit report

```bash
/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 <<'PY'
import re

# Per-tool quality + comment specifications. Keys are (name, occurrence_index) for tools
# where the per-call quality differs; bare name applies to all calls of that tool.
# occurrence_index is 1-based among that tool's calls.

# General rules I observed:
# - Inputs as backticked Path/Query/URL/inline = ✅ friendly
# - Inputs as raw fenced \```json {...}\``` = OK but still JSON
# - Outputs as fenced \```text\``` with actual content = ✅
# - Outputs as raw JSON in \```text\``` = readable but ugly = 😢
# - Outputs as single-line raw JSON (no fence) = 🆘
# - Phantom HTML inside output = 🆘
# - Both input and output as raw JSON dump or scrambled = 🆘
# - Missing/empty rendering = 🚫 or 😢 depending on whether data exists

# Default quality by tool name (applies to every call)
DEFAULT = {
    "tool_search_tool_regex": ("🆘", "Input as pretty JSON in fence; output as single-line raw JSON appended after 'Output:' — not in code fence, hard to read."),
    "tool_search_tool_bm25": ("🆘", "Same shape as tool_search_tool_regex: output is single-line raw JSON, not in a fence — unfriendly to read."),
    "write_file": ("✅", "Path backticked, file contents in fenced code block, output is unified diff plus 'Created … (N bytes)' confirmation."),
    "list_dir": ("😢", "Path backticked; output is JSON-array of entries inside \```text\``` fence — readable but raw, no human-friendly listing."),
    "read_file": ("✅", "Path backticked; output is file contents in \```text\``` fence (or inline error). Clean."),
    "checklist_write": ("✅", "Renders a real Markdown todo list with `[x]`, `[⚡]`, `[ ]` glyphs, then a JSON appendix with status/completion_pct."),
    "diagnostics": ("😢", "No input fields; output is just a raw JSON object dumped into \```text\``` fence — readable but unstructured."),
    "file_search": ("😢", "Pattern backticked; output is raw JSON-array of {path, name, score} in \```text\``` fence — fine for one hit, ugly for many."),
    "grep_files": ("😢", "Pattern backticked; output is the full grep_files JSON envelope (matches[], context_before/after, total_matches) — not the line-prefixed grep style readers expect."),
    "update_plan": ("🆘", "Renders the todo list with '(no description)' for every item — the descriptions ARE in the raw input but the markdown placeholder strips them. Output then re-dumps the full JSON with the real descriptions."),
    "todo_write": ("✅", "Same renderer as checklist_write; clean todo list with state glyphs + JSON appendix."),
    "note": ("😢", "Input as raw fenced JSON ({content, title}); output is one-line 'Note appended to …' — concise but the input would read better as a quoted note body."),
    "task_shell_start": ("✅", "Renders Directory + the command itself in an inline code span on its own line, plus 'Background task started: shell_…' as Output."),
    "task_shell_wait": ("✅", "Input JSON in fence; output is the actual shell stdout in \```text\``` fence — exactly what a reader wants."),
    "code_execution": ("🆘", "Input is fenced JSON whose 'code' field is a single escaped-newline string (unreadable); output is single-line raw JSON envelope with escaped \\n inside stdout — neither input nor output is human-readable."),
    "task_shell_start": ("✅", "Directory backticked + command shown in inline code; 'Background task started: shell_…' as output. Compact and clear."),
    "agent_spawn": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; output is the full agent state JSON (agent_id, nickname, status, model, …) in \```text\``` fence — readable but a one-line summary would help."),
    "agent_list": ("😢", "No input; output is the JSON-array of agent records in \```text\``` fence — same shape as agent_spawn payload."),
    "agent_wait": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; output is multi-line text with embedded sentinel header + agent's SUMMARY markdown — the markdown sub-content renders but the leading sentinel + 'stats: steps=…' line break formatting awkwardly."),
    "agent_result": ("😢", "Same shape as agent_wait: sentinel + agent's structured report + stats line, all inside one \```text\``` fence."),
    "agent_cancel": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; output is full agent state JSON in \```text\``` fence (long objective/result strings)."),
    "git_status": ("✅", "No input; output is `## main...origin/main` plus porcelain `??`/` M` lines inside \```text\``` fence — exactly git's own format."),
    "git_diff": ("😢", "Empty rendering: no input, no output content. The raw call did execute with empty input and returned an empty string — but the markdown gives the reader no indicator (e.g., 'Output: (no changes)'). Looks like the tool didn't run."),
    "git_log": ("✅", "No input; output is per-commit (hash/Author/Date/Subject) inside \```text\``` fence — readable and git-like."),
    "git_show": ("✅", "Input JSON in fence; output is commit header + diff in \```text\``` fence — readable. NOTE: success call leaks unescaped `<tool-use>` tags inside the diff content, which the markdown parser surfaces as 41 phantom tool blocks."),
    "git_blame": ("✅", "Input JSON in fence ({path:…}); output is inline 'git blame failed …' error message. Clean."),
    "web_search": ("😢", "Query backticked; output is the full results envelope ({query, source, count, results:[…]}) as JSON in \```text\``` fence — readable but better rendered as a numbered list."),
    "fetch_url": ("😢", "URL backticked; output is {url, status, content_type, content:'…escaped…'} as JSON in \```text\``` fence — the body is a single escaped JSON string."),
    "review": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence (or absent on the failure case); output is the structured review report (summary, issues[], suggestions[], overall_assessment) as JSON in \```text\``` fence — would render much better as headings."),
    "task_gate_run": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; output is the full gate JSON envelope (gate.summary contains the wc output as `\\n`-escaped text). Readable JSON, ugly stdout."),
    "github_issue_context": ("✅", "Input JSON in fence; output is the inline `gh issue view …` error message."),
    "run_tests": ("😢", "No input; output is JSON envelope (success/exit_code/stdout/stderr/command) in \```text\``` fence — the actual stderr message lives inside an escaped string."),
    "task_create": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; output is a 40+ line JSON dump of the durable task object — readable but verbose; a one-line 'task_… queued' would serve readers better."),
    "task_list": ("😢", "No input; output is JSON envelope ({summary, tasks:[…]}) in \```text\``` fence — readable but a table would help."),
    "task_read": ("🆘", "Input JSON in fence; output is a 200+ line raw JSON dump including streaming reasoning deltas (#7 '\"Reading\"', #8 '\" both\"', …) — the data is all there but completely overwhelms the document."),
    "recall_archive": ("🆘", "Input JSON in fence; output is single-line raw JSON `{\"hits\":[],\"note\":\"…\"}` appended after `Output:` — no fence."),
    "load_skill": ("✅", "Input JSON in fence; success output is the rendered skill markdown (title, description, source path, body, companion files) — ideal. Failure case (call #1) shows the error inline."),
    "finance": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; output is the raw quote JSON ({ticker, price, change, change_percent, …}) in \```text\``` fence."),
    "notify": ("✅", "Input JSON in fence; output is inline `notified: <title>` — compact and clear."),
    "automation_list": ("✅", "No input; output is `Output: []` inline — concise."),
    "pr_attempt_list": ("✅", "No input shown; output is the inline error message — concise."),
    "retrieve_tool_result": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence ({mode, ref}); output is inline error. Clean for an error path; would need a real success case to fully judge."),
    "request_user_input": ("🆘", "Input has the full questions array as a single inline-style JSON object on one line inside the fence (no pretty-printing of nested options[]); output is the answers in \```text\``` fence — input is barely readable."),
    "rlm": ("✅", "Input JSON in fence; output is RLM's own structured report ('RLM report: …', 'Answer: …', '[RLM trace]') inside \```text\``` fence — readable."),
    "validate_data": ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; success output is the validation envelope JSON (valid/format/summary{top_level, entries, keys_preview}) in \```text\``` fence — readable but raw."),
    "edit_file": ("✅", "Path backticked, input rendered as a \```diff\``` block ('-old' / '+new'). Output is unified diff or 'Replaced N occurrence(s)…' in \```text\``` fence."),
    "apply_patch": ("✅", "Input rendered as \```diff\``` block (the actual patch); output is unified diff + success message OR error with file snippet — both useful."),
}

# Per-occurrence overrides (1-based occurrence within that tool name)
OVERRIDES = {
    # First edit_file used old/new (DeepSeek rejected → 'missing required field search')
    # Renderer happened to produce a synthetic diff from old→new; we still see the input.
    ("edit_file", 1): ("✅", "Path backticked; input rendered as \```diff\``` from old→new fields; output is the inline error from DeepSeek."),
    # Second edit_file used search/replace (succeeded). The renderer only knows old/new,
    # so input is COMPLETELY MISSING from the markdown — only Path + Output diff appear.
    ("edit_file", 2): ("😢", "Path backticked + Output diff present, but the input fields (search/replace) are entirely missing from the rendering — the formatter only handles old/new, not search/replace. Reader can't see what was searched for."),
    # First apply_patch failed with a helpful error
    ("apply_patch", 1): ("✅", "Input rendered as \```diff\``` block; output is a \```text\``` fence showing the apply-failure with file snippet and hints — very readable."),
    # Second apply_patch succeeded but dumped a giant JSON envelope
    ("apply_patch", 2): ("😢", "Input rendered as \```diff\``` block; output is the raw `apply_patch` JSON envelope (success/files_applied/hunks_applied/file_summaries/message) in \```text\``` fence — the human-relevant bit ('Applied 1/1 hunks…') is buried."),
    # Validate_data #1 failed (no required field) — only error shown, but input WAS in raw JSON
    ("validate_data", 1): ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; output is the inline error message — fine for failure case."),
    # Git_show first call failed; output is inline error
    ("git_show", 1): ("✅", "Input JSON in fence; output is the inline 'missing required field rev' error."),
    # Git_show second call succeeded — but the output diff contains LITERAL `<tool-use>` HTML
    # strings (the diff is of other markdown files containing tool-use blocks). The markdown
    # parser does not escape them, so 41 phantom tool-use blocks appear in the rendered output.
    ("git_show", 2): ("🆘", "Output contains UNESCAPED `<tool-use>` HTML inside the diff (the commit shows other agent transcripts). The markdown emits 41 phantom tool-use blocks inside this single output — content bleeding into doc structure."),
    # First review call failed (missing target) — only error in output
    ("review", 1): ("✅", "No input fields rendered (input was empty); output is the inline 'missing required field target' error. Clean for the failure case."),
    # First load_skill failed with wrong param name
    ("load_skill", 1): ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; output is the inline error. Fine for failure case."),
    # First validate_data already covered above; second is the success — dump JSON envelope
    ("validate_data", 2): ("😢", "Input JSON in fence; success output is the validation envelope JSON in \```text\``` fence — readable but raw."),
    # Both tool_search_tool_regex/bm25 are uniformly bad
}

# Load pairs
pairs = []
with open("/tmp/pairs.tsv") as f:
    next(f)
    for line in f:
        idx, typ, name, ms, me, ss, se, uid = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
        pairs.append({"idx": int(idx), "type": typ, "name": name,
                      "ms": int(ms), "me": int(me),
                      "ss": int(ss), "se": int(se), "uid": uid})

# Load fake (phantom) tool-uses
fake = []
with open("/tmp/fake_tools.tsv") as f:
    for line in f:
        s, e, name = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
        fake.append({"ms": int(s), "me": int(e), "name": name})

# Per-name occurrence counter
counters = {}
rows = []
for p in pairs:
    counters[p["name"]] = counters.get(p["name"], 0) + 1
    occ = counters[p["name"]]
    key = (p["name"], occ)
    if key in OVERRIDES:
        q, c = OVERRIDES[key]
    else:
        q, c = DEFAULT.get(p["name"], ("?", "(unscored)"))
    rows.append({
        "name": p["name"],
        "occ": occ,
        "md_lines": f"{p['ms']}–{p['me']}",
        "quality": q,
        "data_file": "session-data.json",
        "sd_lines": f"{p['ss']}–{p['se']}",
        "comment": c,
    })

# Append phantom entries
for f in fake:
    rows.append({
        "name": f["name"],
        "occ": "phantom",
        "md_lines": f"{f['ms']}–{f['me']}",
        "quality": "🚫",
        "data_file": "(none)",
        "sd_lines": "—",
        "comment": "Phantom tool-use block — appears in the markdown but has no corresponding raw tool call. Lives inside the `git_show` HEAD output (which diffs other agent transcripts whose markdown contains literal `<tool-use>` tags). The renderer does not escape these tags, so they surface as fake tool-use blocks. Content artifact, not a real invocation.",
    })

# Build report
out = []
out.append("# Tool-use audit — DeepSeek TUI session 2429dd93")
out.append("")
out.append(f"_Generated: 2026-05-13_")
out.append("")
out.append("Audit of every `<tool-use>` block in:")
out.append("")
out.append(f"- Markdown: `.specstory/history/2026-05-13_14-35-59Z-hello-deepseek-tui-tell.md` (4390 lines)")
out.append(f"- Data: `.specstory/debug/2429dd93-45df-4ea2-a42c-cbb56918ff6b/session-data.json` (2444 lines)")
out.append("")
out.append("## Headline numbers")
out.append("")
out.append(f"- **120** `<tool-use>` blocks rendered in the markdown")
out.append(f"- **79** real tool calls in the raw session data (1-to-1 with the 79 markdown blocks that have no leading-space indentation)")
out.append(f"- **41** phantom blocks rendered from unescaped `<tool-use>` text living inside one `git_show` output — these are content bleed from the diff body, not real invocations")
out.append("")
out.append("## Quality counts")
out.append("")
from collections import Counter
counts = Counter(r["quality"] for r in rows)
total = len(rows)
labels = {"✅": "formatted", "😢": "partial", "🆘": "raw/unformatted", "🚫": "missing"}
for q in ["✅", "😢", "🆘", "🚫"]:
    out.append(f"- {q} {labels[q]}: **{counts.get(q, 0)}** / {total}")
out.append("")
out.append("## Per-block audit")
out.append("")
out.append("| # | Tool | MD lines | Quality | Data file | Data lines | Comment |")
out.append("|---:|------|---------:|:-------:|-----------|-----------:|---------|")
for i, r in enumerate(rows, 1):
    occ_suffix = "" if r["occ"] in ("phantom",) else f" (#{r['occ']})"
    if r["occ"] == "phantom":
        occ_suffix = " (phantom)"
    elif r["occ"] == 1 and Counter(p["name"] for p in pairs)[r["name"]] == 1:
        occ_suffix = ""
    name_display = f"`{r['name']}`{occ_suffix}"
    comment = r["comment"].replace("|", "\\|")
    out.append(f"| {i} | {name_display} | {r['md_lines']} | {r['quality']} | {r['data_file']} | {r['sd_lines']} | {comment} |")

out.append("")
out.append("## Notes")
out.append("")
out.append("- **Data file:** `session-data.json` is the SpecStory CLI intermediate; each tool entry has `input`, `output`, and a `formattedMarkdown` field — the latter is what gets pasted into the history `.md`. The upstream DeepSeek raw is at `raw-session.json` (79 `\"type\": \"tool_use\"` entries, identical order).")
out.append("- **Phantom blocks:** All 41 sit inside lines 2371–3008 — that range is the diff body of the `git_show HEAD` call at lines 2252–3022 (`session-data.json` lines 1671–1734). The diff shows other agents' markdown transcripts whose own `<tool-use>` tags pass through unescaped.")
out.append("- **The biggest recurring problem** is JSON-envelope-dumping for outputs: most tools return a structured object that the renderer drops into a \```text\``` fence without unpacking the human-relevant fields. The clean exceptions (read_file, write_file, task_shell_*, git_status, git_log, load_skill, checklist_write, todo_write, rlm, notify) all have purpose-built renderers.")
out.append("- **`update_plan` is uniquely broken**: the markdown todo list shows '(no description)' for every item — the descriptions exist in the raw input but the renderer can't reach them, then the same descriptions appear in the JSON appendix that follows.")
out.append("- **`edit_file` is half-broken**: the renderer assumes the input fields are `old`/`new` (which DeepSeek rejects as the wrong schema). On the call that uses the correct `search`/`replace` schema, the input vanishes entirely from the rendering — reader sees the diff *output* but not what the agent asked for.")

with open("/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/specstory-cli-multi-agent/deepseek-tui/2026-05-13/tool-use-audit.md", "w") as f:
    f.write("\n".join(out) + "\n")

print(f"Wrote audit ({len(rows)} rows) to tool-use-audit.md")
print(f"Quality breakdown: {dict(counts)}")
PY
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 16:24:48Z)**_

Wrote the audit to `tool-use-audit.md` (154 lines, 120-row table). Headline:

- **120** total `<tool-use>` blocks: **79** real (1-to-1 with raw data) + **41** phantom (unescaped `<tool-use>` tags inside a `git_show HEAD` diff that quotes other agent transcripts).
- Quality split: **✅ 27 formatted · 😢 26 partial · 🆘 26 raw · 🚫 41 missing**.

Key findings flagged in the report:

- The dominant problem is **JSON-envelope dumping** — most tool outputs go into a `text` fence as raw JSON instead of unpacking the human-relevant fields. Clean exceptions: `read_file`, `write_file`, `task_shell_*`, `git_status`, `git_log`, `checklist_write`/`todo_write`, `load_skill`, `notify`, `rlm`.
- **`tool_search_tool_regex`/`bm25` outputs aren't even in a code fence** — single-line raw JSON dumped after `Output:` (17 occurrences combined).
- **`update_plan` is uniquely broken**: the todo list renders every item as `(no description)` even though the descriptions are right there in the input.
- **`edit_file` schema mismatch**: the renderer expects `old`/`new` (which DeepSeek's API rejects). On the call that uses the correct `search`/`replace`, the input vanishes from the markdown — only the output diff appears.
- **`git_show HEAD` content bleed**: a single tool output contains 41 unescaped `<tool-use>` HTML fragments, surfacing as phantom tool blocks in the rendered document.
- **`task_read`** dumps 200+ lines of timeline JSON including per-token streaming reasoning deltas — completely overwhelms the document.
